title: malvaceae
intro: The Malvaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly referred to as the mallow or hibiscus family. This family includes approximately 250 genera and over 2,300 species of plants, such as okra, hibiscus, and cotton. Many members of the Malvaceae family are known for their showy flowers and edible fruits, making them a popular choice for gardening and culinary purposes. The diverse range of plants within the Malvaceae family can be found in tropical and temperate regions around the world.
